card.intuition: A stability pattern that prevents a service from repeatedly trying an operation that is likely to fail, allowing the system to recover rather than cascading the failure.
card.analogy: An electrical circuit breaker in your house: if too much power flows (a service is failing), the breaker 'trips' and cuts the power to prevent a fire (crashing your whole system).
when_to_use: Calling external 3rd-party APIs that might be down or slow
Microservice-to-microservice communication where you want to fail fast
Preventing a 'slow local database' from backing up all your web requests
gotchas: The 'Open' state means the service is NOT called at all (fast fail). The 'Half-Open' state allows a few test requests through to see if it's fixed
Don't use it for operations that should always succeed eventually (like writing a critical log); use a Retry queue instead

exercise.prompt: Identify the circuit breaker state based on the behavior.
hints: Closed means the circuit is complete (flowing)
Open means the circuit is broken (blocked)
exercise.test_code:
assert states["Requests flow normally"] == "Closed"
assert states["Requests fail immediately (blocked)"] == "Open"
print("PASS")
